questions.q: In what animal form does Vishnu appear as the avatar Kurma?
questions.explanation: Kurma is the cosmic tortoise form of Vishnu, his second avatar.

questions.q: Of which deity is Kurma an avatar (incarnation)?
questions.explanation: Kurma is the second of the Dashavatara, the ten descents of Vishnu.

questions.q: During which great cosmic event did Kurma support a mountain on his back?
options: The churning of the ocean of milk
questions.explanation: Kurma bore Mount Mandara during the churning of the ocean of milk (Samudra Manthana).

questions.q: Which mountain did Kurma support on his back during the churning of the ocean?
questions.explanation: Kurma supported Mount Mandara, which served as the churning rod.

questions.q: What was the primary purpose of the churning of the ocean that Kurma made possible?
options: To bring the Ganga to earth
To obtain the amrita, the nectar of immortality
questions.explanation: The churning was undertaken to draw forth the amrita, the nectar of immortality.

questions.q: Which two groups churned the ocean of milk together while Kurma supported the mountain?
questions.explanation: The devas and asuras churned the ocean cooperatively, with Kurma bearing the mountain beneath.

questions.q: What problem did Kurma solve by positioning himself beneath Mount Mandara?
options: The mountain was sinking into the ocean
The mountain was too small to churn
questions.explanation: As the churning-mountain threatened to sink, Kurma bore it on his back to steady it.

questions.q: In the standard sequence of the Dashavatara, which avatar of Vishnu is Kurma?
questions.explanation: Kurma is the second avatar, following Matsya the fish.

questions.q: Which avatar of Vishnu immediately precedes Kurma in the classical Dashavatara list?
questions.explanation: Matsya the fish is the first avatar, and Kurma the tortoise is the second.

questions.q: The cosmic ocean that Kurma helped churn is best described as an ocean of what?
questions.explanation: The Kshira Sagara, the ocean churned with Kurma's aid, is the cosmic ocean of milk.

questions.q: Which serpent served as the churning rope wound around Mount Mandara while Kurma supported it?
questions.explanation: The serpent-king Vasuki was used as the rope around Mount Mandara during the churning.

questions.q: In which Puranas is the account of Kurma supporting Mount Mandara prominently described?
options: The Linga and Skanda Puranas only
It appears only in the Rigveda
questions.explanation: The Bhagavata and Vishnu Puranas describe Kurma bearing the churning-mountain.

questions.q: The goddess Lakshmi is said to have emerged from the very ocean that Kurma helped churn. Whose consort is Lakshmi?
questions.explanation: Lakshmi, who arose from the churned ocean, is the consort of Vishnu, whose avatar is Kurma.

questions.q: Which divine physician rose from the ocean bearing the pot of amrita, the goal of the churning Kurma enabled?
questions.explanation: Dhanvantari, the physician of the gods, emerged carrying the pot of amrita.

questions.q: A deadly poison rose first from the churning before the nectar. Which deity is said to have drunk it to save creation?
questions.explanation: Shiva drank the halahala poison that rose during the churning, becoming Nilakantha.

questions.q: What does the name 'Kurma' mean in Sanskrit?
questions.explanation: The Sanskrit word 'kurma' means tortoise, the form Vishnu took for this avatar.

questions.q: The avatar that comes immediately after Kurma in the Dashavatara is which form of Vishnu?
questions.explanation: Varaha the boar is the third avatar, following Kurma the tortoise.

questions.q: When the asuras seized the pot of amrita after the churning, what form did Vishnu take to recover it?
questions.explanation: Vishnu took the enchanting female form of Mohini to recover the amrita for the devas.

questions.q: The wish-fulfilling cow Kamadhenu is counted among the treasures that arose from the ocean Kurma helped churn. What is Kamadhenu?
questions.explanation: Kamadhenu, the wish-fulfilling cow, was among the treasures yielded by the churning.

questions.q: In the context of the Samudra Manthana, what role did Mount Mandara play that required Kurma's support?
questions.explanation: Mount Mandara was the churning rod, and Kurma supported it from below as a pivot.

questions.q: Which of these is a well-known epithet of Vishnu, whose tortoise descent is Kurma?
options: The Creator of the four Vedas
questions.explanation: Vishnu is the Preserver of the Trimurti, and Kurma is one of his preserving descents.

questions.q: In traditional readings, what does the churning of the ocean, made possible by Kurma, most often symbolize?
options: The final dissolution of the cosmos
The creation of the first man and woman
The defeat of the goddess by the demons
The joint effort needed to obtain a precious goal
questions.explanation: The churning is often read as the cooperative striving by which the nectar of immortality was won.

questions.q: There is a Purana named directly after Vishnu's tortoise avatar. What is it called?
questions.explanation: The Kurma Purana is one of the eighteen Mahapuranas, named after the tortoise avatar.

questions.q: On which part of his body does Kurma bear Mount Mandara during the churning of the ocean?
questions.explanation: Kurma supports Mount Mandara upon his back, using his sturdy shell as the pivot.

questions.q: The celestial tree Parijata is named among the treasures of the churning Kurma enabled. What kind of treasure is the Parijata?
questions.explanation: The Parijata, a wish-bestowing flowering tree, was among the treasures of the churning.

questions.q: The radiant gem Kaustubha arose from the churned ocean Kurma supported. Where is the Kaustubha said to reside?
questions.explanation: Vishnu took the Kaustubha gem to adorn his chest, where it shines beside the Srivatsa mark.

questions.q: Which white elephant, later the mount of Indra, is said to have emerged from the churning of the ocean that Kurma supported?
questions.explanation: Airavata, the white elephant who became Indra's vahana, arose from the churning.

questions.q: Which avatar of Vishnu, generally listed first, comes before the tortoise Kurma and is associated with surviving a great deluge?
questions.explanation: Matsya the fish, the first avatar, saved Manu from the deluge and precedes Kurma.

questions.q: The ocean of milk that Kurma helped churn bears which traditional Sanskrit name?
questions.explanation: The cosmic ocean of milk churned with Kurma's aid is called the Kshira Sagara.

questions.q: Within the cosmic progression of the Dashavatara from the waters toward the perfected human, the tortoise Kurma is often interpreted as representing which stage?
options: The destroyer of the present age
An amphibious stage bridging water and land
questions.explanation: As an amphibian following the wholly aquatic Matsya, Kurma is often read as a transitional stage between water and land.
